source_title: Hipposeq: a comprehensive RNA-seq database of gene expression in hippocampal principal neurons
node_relevance_reason: Directly profiles transcriptomes of CA1, CA2, CA3 pyramidal cells and DG granule cells and mossy cells by RNA-seq, identifying cell-class-specific gene expression relevant to node markers.
summary: Hipposeq used RNA-seq to characterize gene expression across major hippocampal excitatory neuron classes: granule cells and mossy cells of the dentate gyrus, and CA3, CA2, and CA1 pyramidal cells, at both dorsal and ventral poles. This transcriptional atlas clarifies the molecular identities of these neuron types and reveals unexpected variation across the dorsoventral axis. The dataset is directly relevant to marker characterisation of all principal cell nodes in scope.
quotes: we used next-generation RNA sequencing (RNA-seq) to produce a quantitative, whole genome characterization of gene expression for the major excitatory neuronal classes of the hippocampus; namely, granule cells and mossy cells of the dentate gyrus, and pyramidal cells of areas CA3, CA2, and CA1
we profiled transcriptomes at both dorsal and ventral poles, producing a cell-class- and region-specific transcriptional description for these populations

source_title: The Dendrites of CA2 and CA1 Pyramidal Neurons Differentially Regulate Information Flow in the Cortico-Hippocampal Circuit
node_relevance_reason: Directly compares CA2 and CA1 pyramidal neurons, characterising dendritic properties, entorhinal inputs, and action potential output, directly informing both ca1_pc and ca2_pc nodes.
summary: CA2 pyramidal neurons receive 5–6-fold larger EPSPs than CA1 pyramidal neurons from entorhinal cortical inputs, and dendritic propagation is more efficient in CA2 than CA1, enabling action potential output from CA2 but not CA1. This study delineates the differential information processing capacities of CA1 versus CA2 pyramidal cells in the cortico-hippocampal circuit. These results are directly relevant to understanding the functional properties of ca2_pc and ca1_pc nodes.
quotes: these inputs produce a 5- to 6-fold larger EPSP at the soma of CA2 compared with CA1 PNs, which is sufficient to drive action potential output from CA2 but not CA1
dendritic propagation is more efficient in CA2 than CA1

source_title: Hippocampome.org, a resource for subicular neuron types and beyond
node_relevance_reason: Hippocampome.org resource covers subicular pyramidal neurons as a focus, with morphological classification, neurotransmitter annotation, and circuit connectivity data directly relevant to subicular_pyramidal_cell node.
summary: Hippocampome.org classifies rodent hippocampal neuron types using axonal/dendritic morphology, neurotransmitter identity, electrophysiology, and molecular markers, with a focus on subicular neurons and entorhinal cortex. This open-access knowledge base documents all principal cell types in scope, including dentate gyrus, CA subfields, subiculum, and entorhinal cortex. This is a primary reference resource for multiple nodes in scope.
quotes: Hippocampome.org uses axonal and dendritic morphology as a foundational approach to classify neurons in the rodent hippocampal formation, including dentate gyrus, Cornu Ammonis, subiculum, and entorhinal cortex

For each identified neuron type, this open access knowledge base annotates essential properties, such as main neurotransmitter, membrane biophysics, firing patterns, molecular expression, and cell counts

source_title: Noncanonical connections between the subiculum and hippocampal CA1
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises subicular pyramidal cell projections to CA1 via backprojection pathway, with both excitatory and inhibitory elements, relevant to subicular_pyramidal_cell node anatomy and connectivity.
summary: The subiculum sends a significant backprojection to CA1 comprising both excitatory and inhibitory elements, challenging the traditional feedforward unidirectional view of hippocampal circuitry. Conclusive anatomical evidence is presented for noncanonical subicular projections to CA1, with implications for modulation of information processing in hippocampal CA1. This is directly relevant to the subicular_pyramidal_cell node and its anatomical connectivity.
quotes: accumulating evidence supports the existence of a significant backprojection pathway comprised of both excitatory and inhibitory elements from the subiculum to CA1
present recent conclusive anatomical evidence for the presence of noncanonical subicular projections to CA1

source_title: Dorsal and ventral mossy cells differ in their axonal projections throughout the dentate gyrus of the mouse hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises hilar mossy cell axon projection patterns using Drd2-Cre and Crlr-Cre mouse lines, directly relevant to hilar_mossy_cell node including DG inner molecular layer specificity.
summary: Glutamatergic hilar mossy cells have axons terminating primarily in the inner molecular layer (IML) of the dentate gyrus, but dorsal mossy cell axons are an exception — they project to the outer molecular layer. Two Cre lines (Drd2-Cre and Crlr-Cre) were used to label mossy cells and their axons, distinguishing dorsal and ventral mossy cell projection differences. This directly characterises the hilar_mossy_cell node axonal anatomy.
quotes: Glutamatergic hilar mossy cells (MCs) have axons that terminate both near and far from their cell body but stay within the DG, making synapses primarily in the molecular layer
The specificity of MC axons for the inner molecular layer (IML) has been considered to be a key characteristic of the DG

source_title: Local and Long-Range Circuit Connections to Hilar Mossy Cells in the Dentate Gyrus
node_relevance_reason: Directly maps local and long-range circuit connections to hilar mossy cells using viral genetic approaches, characterising excitatory inputs from granule cells and interneurons to mossy cells.
summary: Viral genetic and functional circuit mapping quantified inputs to hilar mossy cells in the mouse dentate gyrus, revealing that the great majority of inputs are excitatory inputs from dentate granule cells and a parallel inhibitory pathway. This directly characterises circuit connectivity for the hilar_mossy_cell node and its relationship to dg_granule_cell node. The study also compares mossy cell inputs with those of dentate granule cells.
quotes: Hilar mossy cells are the prominent glutamatergic cell type in the dentate hilus of the dentate gyrus (DG)
The great majority of inputs to mossy cells consist of two parallel inputs from within the DG: an excitatory input pathway from dentate granule cel

source_title: The role of hippocampal mossy cells in novelty detection.
node_relevance_reason: Directly addresses role of hippocampal mossy cells in novelty detection, relevant to hilar_mossy_cell node with discussion of dorsal vs. ventral mossy cell function.
summary: Hilar mossy cells in the hippocampus play roles in novelty detection and contextual memory formation, with differential involvement of dorsal and ventral hippocampal divisions. The hippocampus encodes spatial memory with growing evidence for mossy cell involvement in novelty detection at both dorsal and ventral poles. This is directly relevant to functional characterisation of the hilar_mossy_cell node.
quotes: The hippocampus has been studied for many decades for its largely known roles in encoding spatial memory, and a growing body of evidence indicates a differential involvement of dorsal and ventral hippocampal divisions in novelty detection

source_title: Pioneer glutamatergic cells develop into a morpho-functionally distinct population in the juvenile CA3 hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises pioneer glutamatergic CA3 pyramidal cells in the juvenile hippocampus, with morpho-functional analysis relevant to ca3_pc node early development and connectivity.
summary: Pioneer glutamatergic neurons in the developing CA3 hippocampus form a morpho-functionally distinct population acting as hub neurons for network synchronization. These cells are glutamatergic CA3 pyramidal cells with high connectivity that can trigger network bursts. This directly characterises ca3_pc node glutamatergic identity and early functional properties.
quotes: Pioneer glutamatergic cells develop into a morpho-functionally distinct population in the juvenile CA3 hippocampus
In the adult CA3, blocking fast GABAergic transmission induces the generation of network bursts that can be triggered by the stimulation of single pyramidal neurons

source_title: Age-Dependent Shift of AMPA Receptors From Synapses to Intracellular Compartments in Alzheimer’s Disease: Immunocytochemical Analysis of the CA1 Hippocampal Region in APP/PS1 Transgenic Mouse Model
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises AMPA receptor expression in CA1 hippocampal region, specifically at synapses of CA1 pyramidal cells in APP/PS1 mouse model, relevant to ca1_pc node synaptic properties.
summary: AMPA receptors shift from synapses to intracellular compartments in the CA1 hippocampal region of APP/PS1 Alzheimer's disease mouse model, correlating with synapse loss. Immunolocalization analyses show altered AMPAR subcellular distribution in CA1 pyramidal cell synapses. This directly characterises the synaptic properties of ca1_pc node through AMPAR expression patterns.
quotes: we used immunolocalization techniques to analyze the expression and subcellular distribution of AMPARs in the hippocampal region of APP/PS1 mouse model of AD
Given the predominant roles of synaptic AMPA receptors (AMPARs) in excitatory synaptic transmission in the brain, changes in their dynamic regulation are also implicated in the pathophysiology of AD

source_title: Semilunar Granule Cells: Glutamatergic Neurons in the Rat Dentate Gyrus with Axon Collaterals in the Inner Molecular Layer
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises semilunar granule cells in the dentate gyrus IML with physiology, morphology, and synaptic targets, directly relevant to dg_semilunar_granule_cell_hippocampus node.
summary: Semilunar granule cells (SGCs) are glutamatergic neurons in the rat dentate gyrus inner molecular layer with granule-like morphology and distinct physiological and synaptic properties. Two-photon imaging and patch clamp recordings defined SGC intrinsic physiology and synaptic targets in hippocampal slices. This is the primary characterisation of the dg_semilunar_granule_cell node.
quotes: We used two-photon imaging, infrared-differential interference contrast microscopy and patch clamp recordings from rat hippocampal slices to define the intrinsic physiology and synaptic targets of spiny, granule-like neurons in the IML, termed semilunar granule cells (SGCs)
These neurons resembled dentate

synonyms_found.quote: spiny, granule-like neurons in the IML, termed semilunar granule cells (SGCs)

source_title: Conserved size and periodicity of pyramidal patches in layer 2 of medial/caudal entorhinal cortex
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises EC layer 2 pyramidal and stellate cell types across mammalian species, including calbindin-positive pyramidal patches, directly relevant to ec_layer2_pyramidal_cell and ec_layer2_stellate_cell nodes.
summary: Layer 2 of the medial entorhinal cortex is organised into two distinct cell types — pyramidal (calbindin-positive) and stellate — whose patch periodicity is conserved across five mammalian species. Calbindin-positive pyramidal cell patches are arranged with spatial periodicity linked to grid cell activity. This directly characterises the ec_layer2_pyramidal_cell and ec_layer2_stellate_cell nodes.
quotes: Principal neurons in layer 2 are divided into two distinct cell types, pyramidal and stellate, based on morphology, immunoreactivity, and functional properties
We confirm the existence of patches of calbindin‐positive pyramidal cells across these species, arranged periodically

source_title: Local and Distant Input Controlling Excitation in Layer II of the Medial Entorhinal Cortex
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises four excitatory neuron types in EC layer II (MEC) including pyramidal and stellate cells and their local network connectivity, directly relevant to ec_layer2_stellate_cell and ec_layer2_pyramidal_cell nodes.
summary: Four types of excitatory neurons were identified in layer II of the medial entorhinal cortex (MEC), exhibiting cell-type-specific local excitatory and inhibitory connectivity patterns. This study characterises the local network organisation of EC layer II neurons including pyramidal and stellate cells. It is directly relevant to ec_layer2_stellate_cell_hippocampus and ec_layer2_pyramidal_cell_hippocampus nodes.
quotes: we identified essential components of LII networks in the MEC. We distinguished four types of excitatory neurons that exhibit cell-type-specific local excitatory and inhibitory

source_title: Entorhinal Layer II Calbindin-Expressing Neurons Originate Widespread Telencephalic and Intrinsic Projections
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises calbindin-expressing principal neurons in EC layer II with quantitative hodology, directly relevant to ec_layer2_pyramidal_cell_hippocampus node (Calb1 marker).
summary: Calbindin-expressing (CB+) principal neurons in layer II of the entorhinal cortex originate widespread excitatory telencephalic and intrinsic projections, with half comprising local intrinsic and commissural projections to layers I and II. Systematic quantitative retrograde and anterograde tracing revealed the CB+ population has diverse projections. This directly characterises the ec_layer2_pyramidal_cell_hippocampus node defined by Calb1 (calbindin) expression.
quotes: the layer II CB+ population comprises neurons with diverse, mainly excitatory projections. At least half of them originate local intrinsic and commissural projections which distribute mainly to layer I and II

source_title: Mixed neurotransmission in the hippocampal mossy fibers
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises hippocampal mossy fibers (axons of dentate gyrus granule cells) including glutamatergic and co-transmitter properties, relevant to both dg_granule_cell and hilar_mossy_cell nodes.
summary: Hippocampal mossy fibers, the axons of dentate gyrus granule cells, form glutamatergic synapses on mossy cells and interneurons in the hilus and CA3 pyramidal cells, with recent evidence for transient GABA co-release. The paradigmatic view of mossy fibers as purely glutamatergic has been questioned by evidence for GABA release during development and in the adult after heightened activity. This characterises both dg_granule_cell axonal output and inputs to hilar_mossy_cell and ca3_pc nodes.
quotes: The hippocampal mossy fibers (MFs), the axons of the granule cells (GCs) of the dentate gyrus, innervate mossy cells and interneurons in the hilus on their way to CA3 where they innervate interneurons and pyramidal cells
the paradigmatic view of the MF synapses being only glutamatergic and, thus, excitatory has been questioned

source_title: An update to Hippocampome.org by integrating single-cell phenotypes with circuit function in vivo
node_relevance_reason: Hippocampome.org update integrates in vivo firing dynamics for over 100 hippocampal/entorhinal neuron types, directly covering all principal cell nodes in scope.
summary: An updated Hippocampome.org integrates single-cell phenotyping of firing behavior during ongoing oscillations in vivo for over 100 hippocampal and entorhinal neuron types. This resource covers entorhinal-hippocampal function for all node types in scope including granule cells, mossy cells, CA pyramidal cells, subicular cells, and entorhinal cortex neurons. It provides comprehensive in vivo functional data for all principal cell nodes.
quotes: Single-cell phenotyping of firing behavior during ongoing oscillations in vivo has provided a large body of evidence on entorhinal–hippocampal function
we mined literature to search for information regarding the phase-timing dynamics of over 100 hippocampal/entorhinal neuron types defined in Hippocampome.org

source_title: Acute stress and hippocampal output: exploring dorsal CA1 and subicular synaptic plasticity simultaneously in anesthetized rats
node_relevance_reason: Directly examines synaptic plasticity simultaneously in CA1 and subiculum (major output structures), characterising stress effects on both ca1_pc and subicular_pyramidal_cell nodes.
summary: Acute restraint stress alters synaptic plasticity simultaneously in dorsal CA1 and subiculum, the two major hippocampal output structures. Using dual-channel electrophysiology in anesthetized rats, short- and long-term plasticity were examined in both CA1 and subicular output pathways. This directly characterises the physiological properties of ca1_pc and subicular_pyramidal_cell nodes under stress conditions.
quotes: The Cornu Ammonis‐1 (CA1) subfield and subiculum (SUB) serve as major output structures of the hippocampal formation
we examined the effects of acute restraint stress (30 min) on short‐ and long‐term forms of synaptic plasticity in both CA1 and SUB

source_title: Potential implications of a monosynaptic pathway from mossy cells to adult-born granule cells of the dentate gyrus
node_relevance_reason: Directly reviews mossy cell (MC) circuit function including monosynaptic pathways from mossy cells to adult-born granule cells, directly relevant to hilar_mossy_cell and dg_granule_cell nodes.
summary: Hilar mossy cells (MCs), which are glutamatergic, form monosynaptic connections to adult-born granule cells of the dentate gyrus, innervating both granule cells and local circuit inhibitory neurons. The net effect of MCs on the DG output depends on whether excitatory or inhibitory MCI circuits dominate. This paper characterises the glutamatergic hilar_mossy_cell node and its connectivity to the dg_granule_cell node.
quotes: mossy cells (MCs), a major DG cell type that is glutamatergic and innervates the primary output cells of the DG, the granule cells (GCs)
MCs innervate the GCs as well as local circuit neurons that make GABAergic synapses on GCs

source_title: New insights into the regulation of synaptic plasticity from an unexpected place: hippocampal area CA2.
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises hippocampal CA2 as a region with LTP-resistant Schaffer collateral synapses and unique molecular properties, directly relevant to ca2_pc node.
summary: CA2 area of the hippocampus is uniquely resistant to canonical LTP at Schaffer collateral inputs, sharing features with other plasticity-restricted brain areas. CA2's LTP resistance is anatomically defined and mediated by distinct molecular mechanisms. This directly characterises the ca2_pc node and its atypical plasticity properties.
quotes: Schaffer collateral inputs to hippocampal area CA2 do not readily support canonical activity-dependent long-term potentiation (LTP)
Hippocampal CA2 shares features with other similarly "LTP-resistant" brain areas

source_title: Distinct physiological and developmental properties of hippocampal CA2 subfield revealed by using anti-Purkinje cell protein 4 (PCP4) immunostaining
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises CA2 subfield using Pcp4 (PCP4) as a definitive marker, establishing CA2/CA1 and CA3/CA2 borders, directly relevant to ca2_pc and ec_layer3_pyramidal_cell nodes (Pcp4 marker).
summary: Anti-PCP4 immunostaining effectively delineates the CA2 subfield of the mouse hippocampus, establishing precise CA3/CA2 and CA2/CA1 borders and revealing distinct physiological and developmental properties of CA2. PCP4 immunostaining agrees well with cytoarchitectural definitions of CA2 and reveals unique properties not shared by CA1 or CA3. This is the primary PCP4 marker characterisation for the ca2_pc node.
quotes: Here we report identification of the CA2 region in the mouse by immunostaining with a Purkinje cell protein 4 (PCP4) antibody, which effectively delineates CA3/CA2 and CA2/CA1 borders and agrees well with previous cytoarchitectural definitions of CA2

synonyms_found.quote: immunostaining with a Purkinje cell protein 4 (PCP4) antibody

source_title: Hippocampal cell- and circuit-specific differences in mitochondrial form and function
node_relevance_reason: Directly compares mitochondrial form and function in CA1 vs CA2 circuits, finding CA2 neurons enriched for mitochondrial proteins relative to CA1, directly characterising differences between ca2_pc and ca1_pc nodes.
summary: Hippocampal CA2 neurons are enriched for mitochondrial protein-encoding genes compared to CA1 neurons, suggesting higher metabolic demand. Mitochondrial morphology, protein expression, and calcium levels differ across CA1 and CA2 circuits. This reveals a cell-type-specific metabolic distinction between ca2_pc and ca1_pc nodes.
quotes: Hippocampal CA2 neurons are enriched for genes encoding mitochondrial proteins compared to CA1 neurons, suggesting a difference in metabolic demand across hippocampal circuits

source_title: Laminar Organization of the Entorhinal Cortex in Macaque Monkeys Based on Cell-Type-Specific Markers and Connectivity
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises laminar organisation of EC in macaque, mapping layer II cell types including pyramidal and stellate cells using molecular markers, extending ec_layer2 nodes to primate context.
summary: Cell-type-specific global mapping of entorhinal cortex in macaque monkeys reveals laminar organisation of layer II and other layers, bridging rodent EC neuron type knowledge to primates. Pyramidal and stellate cells in EC layer II are characterised with molecular markers and connectivity in macaques. This extends the ec_layer2_pyramidal_cell and ec_layer2_stellate_cell nodes to a primate context.
quotes: The entorhinal cortex (EC) is a major gateway between the hippocampus and telencephalic structures, and plays a critical role in memory and navigation
here we provide the first cell-type-based global map of EC in macaque monkeys

source_title: Target-selective GABAergic control of entorhinal cortex output
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises target-selective GABAergic control of EC layer II principal cells in MEC, distinguishing neurons projecting outside hippocampus from perforant pathway neurons, directly relevant to ec_layer2_stellate_cell and ec_layer2_pyramidal_cell nodes.
summary: Cannabinoid type 1 receptor-expressing GABAergic basket cells in rat MEC selectively innervate layer II principal cells projecting outside the hippocampus but avoid neighboring cells forming the perforant path to the dentate gyrus. This target-selective inhibition reveals functional segregation of EC layer II principal cell populations. This directly characterises differential connectivity of ec_layer2_stellate_cell versus ec_layer2_pyramidal_cell nodes.
quotes: cannabinoid type 1 receptor–expressing GABAergic basket cells selectively innervated principal cells in layer II of the rat MEC that projected outside the hippocampus but avoided neighboring cells that give rise to the perforant pathway to the dentate gyrus

source_title: Recurrent circuits within medial entorhinal cortex superficial layers support grid cell firing
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises recurrent circuits within MEC superficial layers including layer II pyramidal cells, showing their role in grid cell firing via optogenetic perturbation.
summary: Layer II pyramidal cells in the medial entorhinal cortex form recurrent circuits that support grid cell firing via excitatory and inhibitory feedback. Optogenetic perturbation of locally projecting layer II pyramidal cells affects speed cells, HD cells, and grid cells. This directly characterises the ec_layer2_pyramidal_cell node circuit function.
quotes: optogenetically perturb locally projecting layer II pyramidal cells. We find that sharply tuned HD cells are only weakly responsive while speed, broadly tuned HD cells, and grid cells show pronounced transient excitatory and inhibitory responses

source_title: Spatial Organization of Morpho‐Electric Subtypes of Pyramidal Neuron in the Subiculum
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises morpho-electric subtypes of pyramidal neurons in the subiculum (Sub2 region), providing electrophysiological and morphological classification directly relevant to subicular_pyramidal_cell node.
summary: Subicular pyramidal neurons in sub2 (proximal to mid-distal subiculum) exhibit high morpho-electric heterogeneity with evidence for spatial subdivisions. Whole-cell patch-clamp recordings and morphological reconstructions characterise the electrophysiological and morphological diversity of subicular pyramidal neurons. This directly characterises the subicular_pyramidal_cell_hippocampus node.
quotes: performed a comprehensive electrophysiological and morphological characterization of PYNs by whole‐cell patch‐clamp
The subiculum is a main output structure of the hippocampus, transmitting information from the CA1 to the entorhinal cortex in a spatially structured manner

synonyms_found.quote: a comprehensive electrophysiological and morphological characterization of PYNs

source_title: Hilar mossy cells of the dentate gyrus: a historical perspective
node_relevance_reason: Directly reviews hilar mossy cells from historical perspective, characterising their glutamatergic identity, morphology, vulnerability, and role in DG network, primary reference for hilar_mossy_cell node.
summary: Hilar mossy cells are one of two glutamatergic principal cell types in the dentate gyrus (alongside granule cells), with distinctive appearance, extensive axonal divergence, and vulnerability to excitotoxicity. This historical review covers the morphology, circuit function, and pathological relevance of mossy cells in the DG. It is a primary reference for the hilar_mossy_cell_hippocampus node.
quotes: there are two glutamatergic principal cells instead of one: granule cells, which are the vast majority of the cells in the DG, and the so-called "mossy cells."

The distinctive appearance of mossy cells, the extensive divergence of their axons, and their vulnerability to excitotoxicity relative to granule cells has led to a great deal of interest in mossy cells

source_title: Synaptic and Vesicular Coexistence of VGLUT and VGAT in Selected Excitatory and Inhibitory Synapses
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises VGLUT and VGAT coexistence in mossy fiber terminals (dentate granule cell axons) and CA3 pyramidal cell synapses, relevant to dg_granule_cell NT identity and mossy fiber properties.
summary: VGLUT2 and VGAT coexist in a sizeable vesicle pool in hippocampal mossy fiber terminals, where VGAT immunoisolates also transport glutamate. Postembedding immunogold double labeling reveals that VGLUT1, VGLUT2, and VGAT coexist in mossy fiber terminals of the hippocampus. This directly characterises the glutamatergic identity and co-transmitter properties of dg_granule_cell axons projecting to ca3_pc and hilar_mossy_cell targets.
quotes: VGLUT2 and VGAT, and also VGLUT1 and VGLUT2, coexist in a sizeable pool of vesicles
VGLUT1, VGLUT2, and VGAT coexist in mossy fiber terminals of the h

source_title: Mixed Electrical–Chemical Synapses in Adult Rat Hippocampus are Primarily Glutamatergic and Coupled by Connexin-36
node_relevance_reason: Characterises mixed electrical-chemical synapses (gap junctions) at mossy fiber terminals on CA3 pyramidal cell thorny excrescences, directly relevant to ca3_pc and dg_granule_cell node connectivity.
summary: Mixed electrical-chemical synapses (gap junctions plus glutamatergic chemical synapses) exist at mossy fiber terminals on thorny excrescences of CA3 pyramidal neurons in adult rat hippocampus. These are primarily glutamatergic and coupled by Connexin-36. This directly characterises the glutamatergic connectivity between dg_granule_cell axons and ca3_pc dendritic spines.
quotes: mossy fiber (MF) terminals on thorny excrescences of CA3 pyramidal neurons (CA3pyr), apparently forming glutamatergi
Mixed Electrical–Chemical Synapses in Adult Rat Hippocampus are Primarily Glutamatergic and Coupled by Connexin-36

source_title: Electrophysiological characterization of granule cells in the dentate gyrus immediately after birth
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises electrophysiological properties of dentate gyrus granule cells immediately after birth and their GABAergic-to-glutamatergic developmental transition, directly relevant to dg_granule_cell node.
summary: Dentate gyrus granule cells exhibit a GABAergic phenotype immediately after birth and only later integrate into the classical glutamatergic trisynaptic hippocampal circuit. Whole cell patch-clamp recordings characterise granule cell electrophysiology during this developmental transition. This directly characterises the dg_granule_cell_hippocampus node developmental and physiological properties.
quotes: Granule cells (GCs) in the dentate gyrus are generated mainly postnatally
immediately after birth, GCs exhibit a clear GABAergic phenotype. Only later they integrate the classical glutamatergic trisynaptic hippocampal circuit

source_title: Expression of the AMPA Receptor Subunits GluR1 and GluR2 is Associated with Granule Cell Maturation in the Dentate Gyrus
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises AMPA receptor subunit GluR1 and GluR2 expression as markers of mature dentate gyrus granule cells, directly relevant to dg_granule_cell node molecular identity and maturation.
summary: AMPA receptor subunits GluR1 and GluR2 are expressed in differentiated (mature) granule cells but not in stem cells or immature neurons in the dentate gyrus. These subunits mark granule cell maturation in neonatal and adult dentate gyrus. This characterises the dg_granule_cell node AMPAR expression profile as a maturation marker.
quotes: AMPA receptor subunits GluR1 and GluR2 are expressed in differentiated granule cells, but not in stem cells, in neonatal, and adult dentate gyrus
During granule cell maturation, defined markers are expressed in a highly regulated sequential process

source_title: Running reorganizes the circuitry of one-week-old adult-born hippocampal neurons
node_relevance_reason: Characterises glutamatergic inputs to young adult-born granule cells including inputs from mature DG granule cells, CA1-3 pyramidal cells, and mossy cells, directly relevant to dg_granule_cell, ca1_pc, ca3_pc, and hilar_mossy_cell nodes.
summary: Virus-based anatomical tracing reveals that one-week-old adult-born dentate granule cells receive inputs not only from GABAergic interneurons but also from multiple glutamatergic cell types, including mature dentate granule cells, CA1-3 pyramidal cells, and mossy cells. Running reorganizes this glutamatergic input circuitry. This characterises synaptic connectivity among dg_granule_cell, ca1_pc, ca3_pc, and hilar_mossy_cell nodes.
quotes: very young, one-week-old, new granule cells in male C57Bl/6 mice receive input not only from GABAergic interneurons, but also from multiple glutamatergic cell types, including mature dentate granule cells, area CA1-3 pyramidal cells and mossy cells

source_title: Differential Glutamatergic Inputs to Semilunar Granule Cells and Granule Cells Underscore Dentate Gyrus Projection Neuron Diversity
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises semilunar granule cells (SGCs) and their differential glutamatergic inputs compared to granule cells, directly relevant to dg_semilunar_granule_cell and dg_granule_cell nodes.
summary: Semilunar granule cells (SGCs) receive stronger medial entorhinal cortex and associational synaptic drive than granule cells (GCs), with higher spontaneous excitatory synaptic input frequency and greater dendritic spine density. Dual GC-SGC recordings reveal that SGCs lack short-term facilitation of lateral entorhinal cortex inputs observed in GCs. This directly characterises the dg_semilunar_granule_cell node and its distinction from dg_granule_cell.
quotes: SGCs receive more frequent spontaneous excitatory synaptic inputs than granule cells (GCs)
SGCs receive stronger medial entorhinal cortex and associational synaptic drive but lack short-term facilitation of lateral entorhinal cortex inputs observed in GCs

synonyms_found.quote: Semilunar Granule Cells (SGCs) are sparse dentate gyrus projection neurons

source_title: Hippocampome.org: a knowledge base of neuron types in the rodent hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Primary Hippocampome.org resource characterising 122 neuron types in rodent hippocampal formation including all principal cell nodes in scope with neurotransmitter, morphology, electrophysiology, and molecular markers.
summary: Hippocampome.org characterises 122 neuron types in the rodent hippocampal formation (dentate gyrus, CA3, CA2, CA1, subiculum, and entorhinal cortex) based on neurotransmitter, axonal/dendritic patterns, electrophysiology, and molecular biomarkers. Data are linked to peer-reviewed experimental evidence and reconciled across inconsistent terminologies. This is a primary comprehensive reference for all 13 principal cell nodes in scope.
quotes: Hippocampome.org is a comprehensive knowledge base of neuron types in the rodent hippocampal formation (dentate gyrus, CA3, CA2, CA1, subiculum, and entorhinal cortex)

our extensive literature mining and data reconciliation identified 122 neuron types based on neurotransmitter, axonal and dendritic patterns, synaptic specificity, electrophysiology, and molecular biomark

source_title: Dopaminoceptive D1 and D2 neurons in ventral hippocampus arbitrate approach and avoidance in anxiety
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises dopamine D1 and D2 receptor-expressing neurons in ventral hippocampus including ventral subicular pyramidal cells and CA1 cells, directly relevant to hpc_glu_dopa_receptor_pyramidal node.
summary: Dopaminoceptive D1 and D2 receptor-expressing neurons in mouse ventral hippocampus are transcriptionally distinct, topographically organized across vHipp subfields and cell types, and mediate approach vs. avoidance in anxiety. These include neurons in the ventral subiculum and other vHipp cell types. This directly characterises the hpc_glu_dopa_receptor_pyramidal_hippocampus node (Drd1, Drd2 markers).
quotes: We here study dopaminoceptive neurons in mouse ventral hippocampus (vHipp), molecularly distinguished by their expression of dopamine D1 or D2 receptors
these neurons are transcriptionally distinct and topographically organized across vHipp subfields and cell types. In the ventral subicu

source_title: Anatomical and molecular characterization of dopamine D1 receptor-expressing neurons of the mouse CA1 dorsal hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises dopamine D1 receptor-expressing neurons in mouse dorsal CA1 hippocampus using BAC transgenic mice, directly relevant to hpc_glu_dopa_receptor_pyramidal node (Drd1 marker).
summary: Dopamine D1 receptor-expressing neurons in mouse dorsal CA1 hippocampus are characterised using BAC transgenic EGFP mice, revealing their molecular identity among CA1 interneuron populations. D1R-expressing cells in CA1 dorsal hippocampus include both pyramidal neurons and interneurons. This directly characterises the hpc_glu_dopa_receptor_pyramidal_hippocampus node Drd1 marker expression in CA1.
quotes: Using BAC transgenic mice expressing enhanced green fluorescent protein under the control of D1R promoter, we examined the molecular identity of D1R-containing neurons within the CA1 subfield of the dorsal hippocampus

source_title: Glutamatergic Neurons of the Mouse Medial Septum and Diagonal Band of Broca Synaptically Drive Hippocampal Pyramidal Cells: Relevance for Hippocampal Theta Rhythm
node_relevance_reason: Characterises glutamatergic MS-DBB neurons that synaptically drive hippocampal pyramidal cells via theta rhythm modulation, relevant to CA1/CA3 pyramidal cell inputs but does not characterise the pyramidal cells themselves.
summary: Glutamatergic neurons of the medial septum and diagonal band of Broca (MS-DBB) provide synaptic input to hippocampal pyramidal cells and are relevant to theta rhythmogenesis. Their electrophysiological properties and synaptic connectivity to hippocampal pyramidal cells is characterised using transgenic mice. This paper describes the afferent inputs to CA1/CA3 pyramidal cells rather than the pyramidal cells themselves.
quotes: Neurons of the medial septum and diagonal band of Broca (MS-DBB) provide an important input to the hippocampus and are critically involved in learning and memory
we examined the electrophysiological properties of glutamatergic MS-DBB neurons and tested whether they provide a functional synaptic input to the hippocampus

source_title: Optogenetic Activation of Cajal-Retzius Cells Reveals Their Glutamatergic Output and a Novel Feedforward Circuit in the Developing Mouse Hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Characterises Cajal-Retzius cells' glutamatergic output onto CA1 pyramidal cells and interneurons in developing mouse hippocampus, relevant to glutamatergic circuit inputs to ca1_pc node but focuses on Cajal-Retzius cells not the pyramidal cells themselves.
summary: Optogenetic activation of Cajal-Retzius cells in stratum lacunosum-moleculare reveals glutamatergic EPSCs onto both CA1 pyramidal cells and local interneurons in the developing mouse hippocampus. This characterises a feedforward glutamatergic circuit from Cajal-Retzius cells to CA1 pyramidal neurons. The paper documents glutamatergic signalling received by ca1_pc node rather than characterising the pyramidal cells themselves.
quotes: Light delivered to stratum lacunosum-moleculare triggered EPSCs both on local interneurons and on pyramidal cells

source_title: Effects of serotonin in the hippocampus: how SSRIs and multimodal antidepressants might regulate pyramidal cell function
node_relevance_reason: Directly addresses effects of serotonin on hippocampal pyramidal cell function and SSRI mechanisms, characterising modulation of CA1 pyramidal cell physiology relevant to ca1_pc node.
summary: Serotonin modulates hippocampal pyramidal cell function and synaptic plasticity, with SSRIs and multimodal antidepressants affecting hippocampal region function relevant to major depressive disorder. The review characterises 5-HT receptor expression and function in hippocampus, with focus on how serotonin modulates CA1 and CA3 pyramidal cell activity. This provides functional pharmacology context for ca1_pc and ca3_pc nodes.
quotes: The hippocampus plays an important role in emotional and cognitive processing
the majority of 5-HT receptor subtypes are expressed there

source_title: Molecular Mechanisms of Synaptic Specificity: Spotlight on Hippocampal and Cerebellar Synapse Organizers
node_relevance_reason: Reviews molecular mechanisms of synaptic specificity in hippocampal and cerebellar circuits, with hippocampal data relevant to glutamatergic synapse formation on CA3 pyramidal cells and dentate granule cells.
summary: Recent studies highlight cell-surface proteins as organizers of synaptic specificity in hippocampal circuits, including cerebellar comparisons. Molecular mechanisms of synapse formation and circuit specificity are discussed for hippocampal neurons. This is relevant to characterising the glutamatergic synapse properties of CA principal cell and granule cell nodes.
quotes: Synapses and neural circuits form with exquisite specificity during brain development to allow the precise and appropriate flow of neural information

source_title: Involvement of Adult Hippocampal Neurogenesis in Learning and Forgetting
node_relevance_reason: Reviews adult hippocampal neurogenesis and pattern separation, characterising newly born dentate gyrus granule cells and their maturation, relevant to dg_granule_cell node.
summary: Adult hippocampal neurogenesis involves continuous generation of new granule cells in the dentate gyrus that contribute to pattern separation and forgetting. Newborn neurons integrate into the hippocampal circuit and influence hippocampal-dependent learning and memory. This is relevant to dg_granule_cell_hippocampus node maturation and function.
quotes: Adult hippocampal neurogenesis is a process involving the continuous generation of newborn neurons in the hippocampus of adult animals

source_title: Septo–hippocampal interaction
node_relevance_reason: Characterises septo-hippocampal interaction via glutamatergic, cholinergic, and GABAergic septal projections to CA1 pyramidal neurons, relevant to ca1_pc node afferent inputs and theta rhythmogenesis.
summary: Septo-hippocampal pathways from medial septum regulate CA1 network excitability through glutamatergic, cholinergic, and GABAergic inputs to CA1 pyramidal neurons and interneurons. The feedback loop between medial septum and CA1 involves direct projections onto glutamatergic pyramidal neurons. This characterises extrinsic inputs to ca1_pc node rather than the pyramidal cells themselves.
quotes: Neurons of these three classes project to glutamatergic pyramidal neurons and different subsets of GABAergic neurons in the hippocampal CA1 region

source_title: Unique Properties of NMDA Receptors Enhance Synaptic Excitation of Radiatum Giant Cells in Rat Hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Characterises CA1 radiatum giant cells (RGCs) as a distinct glutamatergic projection neuron with NMDA-dominant excitation, a novel CA1 excitatory cell type distinct from pyramidal cells, relevant to glutamatergic cell diversity in CA1.
summary: CA1 radiatum giant cells (RGCs) are excitatory projection neurons in rat hippocampus with unique NMDA receptor-dependent burst firing in response to glutamatergic synaptic activation, distinct from CA1 pyramidal cells. NMDA receptor activity is the primary driver of RGC synaptic excitation even after blocking non-NMDA receptors. This describes a distinct glutamatergic CA1 cell type relevant to the broader hippocampal glutamatergic cell landscape.
quotes: We used the whole-cell patch-clamp technique in adult hippocampal slices to assess the role of NMDA receptors in synaptic excitation of a recently discovered excitatory projection neuron, the CA1 radiatum giant cell (RGC)
Glutamatergic synaptic activation, even after blocking non-NMDA receptors, fired an NMDA receptor-dependent burst of action potentials in RGCs

source_title: Distribution of the excitatory amino acid receptor subunits GluR2(4) in monkey hippocampus and colocalization with subunits GluR5-7 and NMDAR1
node_relevance_reason: Characterises AMPA/kainate and NMDA receptor subunit distribution in monkey hippocampus including CA1 and dentate gyrus, relevant to glutamate receptor expression in ca1_pc and dg_granule_cell nodes.
summary: Ionotropic glutamate receptor subunits GluR2/4 (AMPA/kainate) colocalize with GluR5-7 and NMDAR1 in monkey hippocampus, with cell-type specific patterns in CA1, CA3, and dentate gyrus. Immunohistochemical analysis reveals cell-type-specific receptor subunit distribution patterns across hippocampal principal cells. This characterises glutamate receptor properties of ca1_pc and dg_granule_cell nodes in a primate context.
quotes: Ionotropic excitatory amino acid (EAA) receptors are divided pharmacologically into three categories termed NMDA, AMPA/kainate, and high affinity kainate receptors

source_title: The Acute Effects of Amyloid-Beta1–42 on Glutamatergic Receptor and Transporter Expression in the Mouse Hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Characterises acute effects of amyloid-beta on glutamatergic receptor and transporter expression in mouse hippocampus, relevant to glutamatergic receptor properties in ca1_pc and ca3_pc nodes.
summary: Amyloid-beta 1-42 acutely alters glutamatergic receptor and transporter expression in the mouse hippocampus, with implications for Alzheimer's disease pathology. This study documents changes in AMPA, NMDA, and glutamate transporter expression in hippocampal subfields including CA1 and CA3. The findings are relevant to glutamatergic properties of ca1_pc and ca3_pc nodes in a disease context.
quotes: Glutamate is the main excitatory neurotransmitter in the brain and plays an essential role in the normal function and excitability of neuronal networks

source_title: Characterization of hippocampal Cajal-Retzius cells during development in a mouse model of Alzheimer's disease (Tg2576)
node_relevance_reason: Characterises hippocampal Cajal-Retzius cells using reelin (Reln) marker during development in Alzheimer's disease mouse model, with Reln directly relevant to the ec_layer2_stellate_cell node marker.
summary: Cajal-Retzius cells in the hippocampal marginal zone express reelin (Reln) and show age-dependent reduction in both wild-type and Alzheimer's disease (Tg2576) mice via apoptosis. Reelin is a defining marker of Cajal-Retzius cells and is also listed as a key marker for the ec_layer2_stellate_cell_hippocampus node. This paper characterises a Reln-expressing cell population distinct from the EC layer II stellate cells but sharing the same marker.
quotes: Cajal-Retzius cells are reelin-secreting neurons in the marginal zone of the neocortex and hippocampus
numerous reelin-positive neurons were positive for activated caspase 3 in Tg2576 mice

source_title: A Novel Type of GABAergic Interneuron Connecting the Input and the Output Regions of the Hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Characterises a novel GABAergic interneuron connecting dentate gyrus outer molecular layer to subiculum, relevant to connectivity between DG and subicular_pyramidal_cell nodes.
summary: A novel GABAergic interneuron type in the dentate gyrus outer molecular layer projects from the dentate gyrus to the subiculum across the hippocampal fissure, characterised by whole-cell patch-clamp and biocytin filling. This interneuron connects DG input to subicular output regions. The paper is relevant to understanding GABAergic inputs to the subicular_pyramidal_cell_hippocampus node from the DG.
quotes: a novel type of GABAergic interneuron located in the outer molecular layer (OML) of the rat dentate gyrus with a long-range forward projection from the dentate gyrus to the subiculum across the hippocampal fissure

source_title: Neuroplastin deletion in glutamatergic neurons impairs selective brain functions and calcium regulation: implication for cognitive deterioration
node_relevance_reason: Characterises neuroplastin deletion in glutamatergic neurons of hippocampal-cortical pathways affecting cognitive functions, relevant to glutamatergic neuron properties in CA regions and entorhinal cortex.
summary: Selective deletion of neuroplastin (Np) in glutamatergic neurons using Emx1Cre impairs brain function and calcium regulation in hippocampal-cortical pathways, with neuroplastin expressed in glutamatergic neurons of the hippocampus and entorhinal cortex. This characterises neuroplastin as a molecular component of glutamatergic CA and EC neurons. The findings are relevant to the molecular identity of hippocampal principal cell and entorhinal cortex nodes.
quotes: Np-deficient mice display complex cognitive deficits and reduced levels of Plasma Membrane Ca2+ ATPases (PMCAs)
We show abundant expression and conserved cellular and molecular features of Np in glutamatergic neurons in human hippocampal-cortical pathways

source_title: Structure of Excitatory Synapses and GABAA Receptor Localization at Inhibitory Synapses Are Regulated by Neuroplastin-65*
node_relevance_reason: Characterises neuroplastin-65 regulation of excitatory synapse structure and GABAAR localisation at inhibitory synapses in hippocampal neurons, relevant to glutamatergic synapse properties of CA principal cells.
summary: Neuroplastin-65 regulates excitatory synapse contact and stability and GABAAR alpha2 subunit localisation at inhibitory synapses in hippocampal neurons. This cell adhesion molecule influences the excitatory-inhibitory ratio at hippocampal synapses. The findings are relevant to synapse formation on hippocampal glutamatergic principal cells including CA pyramidal neurons.
quotes: The cell adhesion molecule neuroplastin-65 regulates contact and stability of excitatory synapses and localization of GABAAR α2 subunits at inhibitory synapses

source_title: Synaptic transmission onto hippocampal glial cells with hGFAP promoter activity
node_relevance_reason: Characterises hippocampal glial cells (GFAP+) receiving glutamatergic synaptic input from Schaffer collaterals, with S100b and GFAP markers relevant to the hpc_glur_cell node (S100b, Cspg4 markers).
summary: Hippocampal glial cells with hGFAP promoter activity receive glutamatergic synaptic inputs from Schaffer collaterals, forming synapse-like structures with EGFP-positive postsynaptic compartments. Sub-threshold Schaffer collateral stimulation evokes postsynaptic responses in a subpopulation of GFAP-positive glial cells. This is relevant to the hpc_glur_cell_hippocampus node which has glial markers (S100b, Cspg4) alongside glutamatergic identity.
quotes: Sub-threshold stimulation to Schaffer collaterals resulted in stimulus-correlated, postsynaptic responses in a subpopulation of EGFP-positive cells studied with the patch-clamp technique in acute slices

source_title: Anatomy and function of the fornix in the context of its potential as a therapeutic target
node_relevance_reason: Reviews fornix anatomy and DBS for Alzheimer's disease with limited hippocampal cell-type specificity; mentions hippocampal principal cells only in circuit context.
summary: The fornix connects limbic circuitry nodes including hippocampal subfields, and deep brain stimulation of the fornix is being investigated for Alzheimer's disease treatment. This paper reviews fornix anatomy and function in the context of DBS therapy. It mentions hippocampal principal neurons only in the context of broader circuit anatomy and is not cell-type specific.
quotes: The fornix is a white matter bundle located in the mesial aspect of the cerebral hemispheres, which connects various nodes of a limbic circuitry and is believed to play a key role in cognition and episodic memory recall

source_title: Neurosteroids Involvement in the Epigenetic Control of Memory Formation and Storage
node_relevance_reason: Reviews epigenetic control of memory formation via neurosteroids in the hippocampus; mentions hippocampal neurons in general context without cell-type specificity.
summary: Neurosteroids influence epigenetic control of memory formation via DNA methylation and histone modification in hippocampal neurons including CA1. The review addresses hippocampal circuit function in memory at a general level. It mentions pyramidal cells indirectly in the context of synaptic plasticity without cell-type specific detail.
quotes: Memory is our ability to store and remember past experiences; it is the result of changes in neuronal circuits of specific brain areas as the hippocampus

source_title: Neuronal Transplantation for Alzheimer’s Disease and Prospects for Generating Exogenic Neurons as a Source of Cells for Implantation
node_relevance_reason: Focuses on neuronal transplantation strategies for Alzheimer's disease; mentions hippocampal neurons in the context of cell replacement therapy but without cell-type specificity.
summary: Neuronal transplantation using blastocyst complementation is proposed as an approach to generate exogenic neurons for Alzheimer's disease therapy. The paper discusses cell replacement in hippocampal circuits at a general level without characterising specific node types. Relevance is indirect as it discusses hippocampal principal neuron replacement.
quotes: Cellular transplantation of healthy exogenic neurons to replace and restore neuronal cell function has previously been explored in AD animal models

source_title: Excitatory inhibitory imbalance underlies hippocampal atrophy in individuals with 22q11.2 Deletion Syndrome with psychotic symptoms
node_relevance_reason: Studies E/I balance in hippocampus of 22q11.2 deletion syndrome using MRS; mentions glutamate levels in hippocampus without cell-type specificity.
summary: MRS measurements of glutamate+glutamine and GABA in hippocampus of 22q11.2 deletion carriers reveal abnormal E/I balance predating psychotic symptoms. The hippocampus is one of three brain regions measured for neurotransmitter levels. Cell-type specificity is absent and relevance to the nodes in scope is indirect.
quotes: Glx (glutamate + glutamine) and GABA+ concentrations were estimated in the anterior cingulate cortex (ACC), superior temporal gyrus (STG) and hippocampus

source_title: Long-Term Estrogen Receptor Beta Agonist Treatment Modifies the Hippocampal Transcriptome in Middle-Aged Ovariectomized Rats
node_relevance_reason: Studies estrogen receptor beta effects on hippocampal transcriptome in ovariectomized rats; mentions hippocampal neurons generally without cell-type specificity relevant to nodes in scope.
summary: Long-term ERβ agonist DPN treatment modifies the hippocampal transcriptome in middle-aged ovariectomized rats, including regulation of genes in hippocampal formation subfields. The hippocampal formation is studied at a transcriptomic level without cell-type specificity. Relevance to specific glutamatergic node types is indirect.
quotes: Selective ERβ agonists also influence hippocampal functions, although their downstream molecular targets and mechanisms are not known

source_title: Glutamate Acting on AMPA But Not NMDA Receptors Modulates the Migration of Hippocampal Interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Studies AMPA receptor-mediated migration of hippocampal GABAergic interneurons; mentions hippocampal pyramidal cells only in contrast to interneuron migration mechanisms.
summary: AMPA but not NMDA receptors modulate the migration of GABAergic hippocampal interneurons, while NMDA receptors modulate pyramidal cell migration. Corticohippocampal organotypic explants are used to compare migration of excitatory versus inhibitory neurons. The reference to pyramidal cell migration is brief and not the main focus.
quotes: Paracrine GABA and glutamate acting, respectively, on GABAA and NMDA receptors modulate the migration of hippocampal pyramidal cells

source_title: Developmental Profile, Morphology, and Synaptic Connectivity of Cajal–Retzius Cells in the Postnatal Mouse Hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Studies Cajal-Retzius cell development in postnatal hippocampus; Reln is listed as a marker for ec_layer2_stellate_cell but Cajal-Retzius cells are distinct from EC layer II stellate cells.
summary: Cajal-Retzius cells in the postnatal mouse hippocampus have non-uniform distribution across subfields with region-specific postnatal decline in the CXCR4-EGFP mouse model. Their morphological and synaptic properties are characterised. Reln is a shared marker with ec_layer2_stellate_cell but these are distinct cell types.
quotes: Cajal–Retzius (CR) cells are early generated neurons, involved in the assembly of developing neocortical and hippocampal circuits

source_title: Synaptic Membrane Glycoproteins gp65 and gp55 Are New Members of the Immunoglobulin Superfamily*
node_relevance_reason: Characterises synaptic membrane glycoproteins gp65/gp55 (neuroplastin family) at hippocampal synapses; relevant to synaptic membrane structure but without hippocampal cell-type specificity.
summary: Synaptic membrane glycoproteins gp65 and gp55 are Ig superfamily members expressed at hippocampal synapses with similarity to neurothelin/basigin. Their molecular characterisation is via cDNA screening and antibody validation. These proteins are expressed at hippocampal synapses but cell-type specificity within hippocampus is not described.
quotes: Glycoproteins gp65 and gp55 are major components of synaptic membranes prepared from rat forebrain
